refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable measurement and reporting of energy consumption per unit of work output.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) the ability to measure/report energy efficiency metrics, and (2) the achievement of a target value. The latter is a performance constraint on the system's operation, not a functional requirement of the system itself. The refined FR focuses on the functional capability—providing visibility into energy efficiency—which is solution-neutral and measurable. Achievement of the energy_per_ton ≤ E_target acceptance criterion is a system-level performance requirement that emerges from correct design of propulsion, hydraulics, and operational control DPs, not from a single FR.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that the NGHE solution can capture energy input (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tons moved, distance, cycles) over a defined evaluation period, and produce a report showing energy_per_ton. Verify the measurement chain is traceable and repeatable across multiple evaluation runs.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall measure total energy consumed during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall measure total work output (e.g., mass moved, distance traveled, or equivalent unit) during operation.
FR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall calculate and report the ratio of energy consumed to work output.

candidate_dps: DP1: Instrument energy input (fuel/electricity) at source; instrument work output (tonnage moved) at point of delivery; compute ratio offline post-evaluation period

DP2: Embed real-time energy and tonnage sensors with local data aggregation; stream normalized metrics to central analytics engine; compute and report energy_per_ton continuously with periodic validation
DP3: Decompose energy consumption into sub-processes (transport, loading, unloading); assign work output proportionally to each; compute energy_per_ton per process and aggregate
DP4: Establish energy baseline from historical operational data; measure incremental energy consumption and work output during evaluation period; compute delta energy_per_ton relative to baseline
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Embed real-time energy and tonnage sensors with local data aggregation; stream normalized metrics to central analytics engine; compute and report energy_per_ton continuously with periodic validation

potential_sources: Sensor calibration drift across heterogeneous equipment types may require independent per-unit validation protocols (mitigated by local aggregation layer)
Central analytics engine creates single point of dependency for metric computation; recommend stateless design to allow horizontal scaling
Real-time streaming introduces temporal coupling between sensor networks and analytics; recommend buffering and eventual consistency semantics
Work output measurement (tonnage) may couple to specific loading/unloading equipment; recommend abstract interface for work-unit definition to support multiple equipment types
coupling_check.notes: DP2 selected because it decouples energy measurement (source-agnostic) from work output measurement (equipment-agnostic) via a logical aggregation layer. Continuous reporting enables early detection of efficiency drift. Periodic validation gates acceptance against E_target. DP1 is too coarse (post-hoc only, no operational feedback). DP3 introduces process-level coupling that complicates attribution. DP4 assumes stable baseline, risky in dynamic worksites.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Energy source(s) and work output mechanisms are measurable via standard instrumentation (fuel flow, electrical current, load cells, GPS distance, or equivalent). RISK: Non-standard or legacy equipment may lack accessible measurement points; mitigation: pre-evaluation site survey and adapter design.

ASSUMPTION: Evaluation period is long enough to average transient operational variability and weather effects. RISK: Short evaluation windows may yield noisy energy_per_ton estimates; mitigation: define minimum evaluation duration and statistical confidence thresholds.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan permits installation of sensors and data collection infrastructure without operational disruption. RISK: Safety constraints may restrict sensor placement or require additional guarding; mitigation: early coordination with safety officer.

ASSUMPTION: Central analytics engine has reliable network connectivity to sensor nodes. RISK: Intermittent connectivity or latency may cause data loss or delayed reporting; mitigation: implement local buffering and offline-capable aggregation.

ASSUMPTION: Energy_per_ton metric is sufficient to characterize efficiency; does not account for distance, terrain, or load variability. RISK: Acceptance criteria may be gamed by optimizing for tonnage without regard to operational context; mitigation: define supplementary KPIs (e.g., energy per ton-km, energy per ton-elevation-gain).

ASSUMPTION: Sensor accuracy and calibration are maintained throughout evaluation period. RISK: Drift or failure of sensors invalidates measurements; mitigation: implement continuous self-check and periodic recalibration protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR to explicitly state: (1) Measurement granularity (per-shift, per-load, real-time aggregate?); (2) Acceptable sensor accuracy tolerance (±5%, ±10%?); (3) Definition of 'work output' (tonnage only, or tonnage × distance × elevation?); (4) Handling of idle/non-productive time (exclude or include in energy_per_ton denominator?); (5) Validation method for acceptance (single evaluation run, multiple runs, statistical confidence level?). These clarifications will constrain DP selection and reduce risk of metric ambiguity during implementation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized real-time energy and tonnage monitoring system encompassing sensor integration, local aggregation logic, streaming pipeline, analytics engine, and continuous KPI computation (energy_per_ton)

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Stream normalized energy and tonnage metrics from embedded sensors and local aggregation to central analytics engine; periodic validation reports

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication for sensor data ingestion from Vehicle and transmission of computed energy_per_ton KPIs and alerts

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive operational context (mission parameters, load profiles) to contextualize energy and tonnage normalization

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest cohesion: DP2 is fundamentally an analytics and KPI reporting system. Sensor embedding and local aggregation are integral to the NGHE energy-efficiency mission. Central analytics engine, normalization, and continuous energy_per_ton computation are core NGHE responsibilities. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry (out) and Network Link (inout) cross boundary; Tasking (in) provides operational context without requiring DP logic distribution.

Poor containment: Vehicle is a physical platform, not an analytics system. Embedding sensors is appropriate, but local aggregation logic, streaming pipeline, and central analytics engine belong in NGHE Solution. DP2 would diffuse significantly: analytics logic would leak into Vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling between platform and analytics. Violates separation of concerns.

Very poor containment: Teleoperation is a control/command interface, not a data analytics system. Energy and tonnage monitoring is orthogonal to remote operation. DP2 would diffuse extensively across Vehicle, NGHE Solution, and Network Link. No logical cohesion.

Poor containment: Safety Services focuses on hazard detection and alert generation, not energy efficiency analytics. While energy_per_ton could inform safety thresholds, the core DP (sensor aggregation, streaming, KPI computation) is not a safety function. DP2 would diffuse into NGHE Solution and Vehicle. Misaligned ownership.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP2 is optimally contained within NGHE Solution because: (1) real-time energy and tonnage monitoring is the primary mission of NGHE; (2) sensor embedding and local aggregation are implementation details of the energy-efficiency strategy; (3) the central analytics engine and continuous KPI computation are core NGHE responsibilities; (4) external interfaces (Telemetry, Network Link, Tasking) are well-defined and minimal, indicating low coupling; (5) ownership is unambiguous—NGHE Solution owns the end-to-end energy monitoring and reporting pipeline.
containment_risks: Sensor firmware on Vehicle may require tight synchronization with NGHE aggregation logic; mitigate via well-defined Network Link protocol and versioning strategy.
Normalization logic depends on operational context (load, mission type); Tasking interface must provide sufficient metadata to avoid DP diffusion into Teleoperation or Safety Services.
Periodic validation may require feedback loops with Vehicle or Safety Services; ensure validation logic remains in NGHE Solution and only consumes validated data from external modules.

Scaling analytics engine for high-frequency sensor streams may require infrastructure decisions (edge vs. cloud); keep architectural boundary stable by abstracting Network Link as a service interface.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP2 directly addresses FR by specifying a complete solution path: embedded sensors capture energy and tonnage data; local aggregation normalizes raw signals; central analytics engine computes energy_per_ton continuously; periodic validation ensures measurement integrity. The DP is solution-agnostic (does not prescribe specific sensor brands or cloud platforms) yet sufficiently concrete to guide implementation. Traceability to acceptance criterion (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) is explicit.

diffusion_risks: Sensor firmware synchronization: Vehicle embeds sensors but NGHE Solution owns aggregation logic. Tight coupling risk if firmware updates are not versioned and decoupled via Network Link protocol. Mitigation: enforce strict API contract on Network Link; version sensor firmware independently from NGHE analytics.

Normalization logic dependency on operational context: Tasking interface must supply load profiles and mission parameters to NGHE Solution for energy normalization. If Tasking metadata is incomplete or inconsistent, normalization logic may diffuse into Teleoperation or Safety Services to infer missing context. Mitigation: define Tasking schema explicitly; make operational context a first-class input to NGHE analytics.

Periodic validation feedback loops: Validation may require cross-module communication (e.g., Vehicle sensor health checks, Safety Services threshold alignment). Risk of validation logic leaking into external modules. Mitigation: keep validation logic centralized in NGHE Solution; external modules provide only data/status, not validation decisions.

Analytics infrastructure scaling: High-frequency sensor streams may require edge processing or cloud offload decisions. Risk of DP logic distribution across edge and central nodes if architectural boundary is not abstracted. Mitigation: define Network Link as a service interface; keep DP logic location-agnostic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 0.92 containment score with 0.08 diffusion. DP2 is optimally scoped: sensor embedding (Vehicle responsibility) and local aggregation (NGHE responsibility) are cleanly separated via Network Link. Central analytics engine, normalization, and energy_per_ton computation are unambiguously owned by NGHE Solution. External interfaces (Telemetry out, Network Link inout, Tasking in) are minimal and well-defined, indicating low coupling. No logical cohesion exists for DP2 in Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Ownership is clear and unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Acceptance criterion (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) is directly measurable via NGHE Solution outputs. Telemetry interface streams normalized metrics; periodic validation reports provide audit trail. Measurement traceability is clear: embedded sensors → local aggregation → central analytics → energy_per_ton KPI. Worksite safety plan and operational policies are inputs to Tasking interface (operational context), ensuring compliance is factored into normalization. Feasibility is high provided Network Link protocol is reliable and sensor calibration is maintained.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR allocation to IM is unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns end-to-end energy monitoring and reporting. Sensor embedding is a Vehicle responsibility (physical integration), but data ownership and analytics ownership reside in NGHE Solution. Tasking provides operational context without requiring DP logic distribution. Telemetry and Network Link are well-defined service interfaces. No ambiguity in module boundaries or responsibility handoffs. Allocation supports independent development and testing of NGHE analytics logic.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Network Link protocol specification: define sensor data schema, streaming frequency, error handling, and versioning strategy to prevent tight coupling between Vehicle firmware and NGHE aggregation logic.

Explicitly define Tasking input schema: specify required operational context fields (load profile, mission type, environmental conditions) to ensure normalization logic has sufficient metadata and does not diffuse into external modules.

Document validation logic ownership: clarify that periodic validation is a NGHE Solution responsibility; external modules (Vehicle, Safety Services) provide only data/status inputs, not validation decisions.
Abstract analytics infrastructure as a service: define Network Link as a location-agnostic service interface to support future edge/cloud scaling decisions without DP logic diffusion.
Establish sensor calibration and maintenance protocol: ensure embedded sensors remain synchronized with NGHE normalization assumptions; document calibration schedule and drift detection logic.
Add traceability matrix: map FR → DP2 → IM (NGHE Solution) → acceptance criterion (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) to support verification planning and test case derivation.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence and clear ownership allocation to NGHE Solution. However, the gate results expose four critical decomposition points that warrant next-level sub-FR derivation to manage implementation risk and ensure safety criticality is properly addressed: (1) Network Link protocol reliability and sensor data integrity (DP-IM diffusion risk: firmware synchronization), (2) Tasking input schema completeness and operational context sufficiency (DP-IM diffusion risk: normalization logic dependency), (3) Validation logic centralization and external module data/status boundaries (DP-IM diffusion risk: feedback loops), and (4) Analytics infrastructure location-agnostic abstraction for edge/cloud scaling (DP-IM diffusion risk: infrastructure scaling). Given high safety criticality, decomposing these concerns into explicit sub-FRs ensures that coupling risks are surfaced early and architectural boundaries are validated before detailed design. Stopping at this level would defer critical interface and protocol decisions to implementation, increasing rework cost and safety verification burden.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2.1: Ensure reliable and versioned sensor data transmission from Vehicle to NGHE Solution via Network Link protocol, with error detection, retry logic, and firmware version tracking to prevent tight coupling and enable independent sensor firmware updates.

FR2.2: Accept and normalize operational context (load profile, mission type, environmental conditions, worksite safety constraints) from Tasking interface with schema validation to ensure energy normalization logic has complete metadata and does not diffuse into external modules.

FR2.3: Perform periodic validation of energy measurement integrity (sensor calibration drift, aggregation logic correctness, normalization assumptions) with centralized validation decision logic and external module data/status inputs only, to prevent validation logic leakage.

FR2.4: Abstract analytics infrastructure (edge vs. central processing, cloud offload decisions) as a location-agnostic service interface to support future scaling without distributing DP logic across edge and central nodes.

FR2.5: Maintain sensor calibration and synchronization with NGHE normalization assumptions through documented calibration schedule, drift detection, and maintenance protocol to ensure measurement traceability and safety compliance.

revise.why: DP2 is logically sound but requires explicit sub-DP specifications for each decomposed FR to formalize the five critical architectural decisions identified in gate results: (1) Network Link protocol specification (schema, frequency, error handling, versioning), (2) Tasking input schema definition (required operational context fields), (3) Validation logic ownership and external interface boundaries, (4) Analytics infrastructure abstraction layer, and (5) Sensor calibration and maintenance protocol. These sub-DPs will guide implementation and reduce diffusion risk during detailed design. Revising DP2 to include these sub-DP pointers ensures traceability and prevents architectural decisions from being deferred to code-level choices.

assumptions: Network Link protocol can be designed with sufficient abstraction to decouple Vehicle firmware from NGHE analytics logic.
Tasking interface can provide complete operational context (load profile, mission type, environmental conditions, safety constraints) required for energy normalization.
Sensor calibration and maintenance can be performed on a predictable schedule without requiring real-time recalibration logic in NGHE Solution.
Analytics infrastructure can be abstracted as a service interface to support both edge and central processing without DP logic distribution.
Validation logic can be centralized in NGHE Solution with external modules providing only data/status inputs, not validation decisions.
Safety criticality is primarily addressed through measurement integrity (FR2.3) and operational context completeness (FR2.2); no additional safety-specific FRs are required at this level.
open_risks: Sensor firmware synchronization: Vehicle embeds sensors but NGHE Solution owns aggregation logic. If Network Link protocol is not versioned and decoupled, firmware updates may break data schema compatibility. Mitigation: enforce strict API contract on Network Link; version sensor firmware independently.

Normalization logic dependency on operational context: If Tasking metadata is incomplete or inconsistent, normalization logic may diffuse into Teleoperation or Safety Services. Mitigation: define Tasking schema explicitly; make operational context a first-class input.

Periodic validation feedback loops: Validation may require cross-module communication (Vehicle sensor health, Safety Services threshold alignment). Risk of validation logic leaking into external modules. Mitigation: keep validation logic centralized; external modules provide only data/status.

Analytics infrastructure scaling: High-frequency sensor streams may require edge processing or cloud offload decisions. Risk of DP logic distribution across edge and central nodes. Mitigation: define Network Link as a service interface; keep DP logic location-agnostic.

Sensor calibration drift: Embedded sensors may drift over time, invalidating normalization assumptions. If calibration schedule is not enforced, measurement integrity may degrade. Mitigation: establish sensor calibration protocol; implement drift detection logic.

Safety criticality verification: Energy monitoring is safety-critical (worksite safety plan compliance). Validation logic must be auditable and traceable. Risk: if validation is not centralized, audit trail may be incomplete. Mitigation: document validation logic ownership; ensure periodic validation reports are comprehensive.
evidence_needed: Network Link protocol specification document: define sensor data schema, streaming frequency, error handling, versioning strategy, and API contract to prevent tight coupling.
Tasking input schema definition: specify required operational context fields (load profile, mission type, environmental conditions, worksite safety constraints) with validation rules.
Validation logic design document: clarify NGHE Solution ownership of validation decisions; define external module data/status inputs; specify validation report format and audit trail.
Analytics infrastructure abstraction design: define service interface for edge/central processing decisions; ensure DP logic remains location-agnostic.
Sensor calibration and maintenance protocol: document calibration schedule, drift detection logic, maintenance procedures, and synchronization with NGHE normalization assumptions.
Traceability matrix: map FR2.1-FR2.5 → sub-DPs → IMs → acceptance criteria to support verification planning and test case derivation.

Safety criticality assessment: confirm that energy monitoring compliance with worksite safety plan is addressed through FR2.2 (operational context) and FR2.3 (validation); identify any additional safety-specific requirements.
Interface contract specifications: formalize Network Link, Tasking, and Telemetry interfaces with error handling, versioning, and backward compatibility strategies.
